test.command: source /home/dragon/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use 22 >/dev/null && cd /data/other/HaloWebUI && npx vitest run && cd /data/other/HaloWebUI/backend && ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest open_webui/test/unit/test_mcp.py -q

typecheck.command: source /home/dragon/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use 22 >/dev/null && cd /data/other/HaloWebUI && (npm run check > /tmp/halo-mission-typecheck.out 2>&1 || true) && python3 -c "from pathlib import Path; import sys; targets=['src/routes/auth/+page.svelte','src/routes/+layout.svelte','src/lib/apis/auths/index.ts','src/lib/apis/auths/index.test.ts','src/routes/auth/guest-flow.test.ts','src/lib/components/layout/Sidebar/UserMenu.svelte','src/lib/components/layout/Overlay/AccountPending.svelte','backend/open_webui/routers/auths.py','backend/open_webui/config.py','backend/open_webui/main.py']; output=Path('/tmp/halo-mission-typecheck.out').read_text(); hits=[line for line in output.splitlines() if any(t in line for t in targets)]; print('\n'.join(hits)) if hits else print('Scoped mission typecheck passed for currently touched guest-mode files.'); sys.exit(1 if hits else 0)"

lint.command: source /home/dragon/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use 22 >/dev/null && cd /data/other/HaloWebUI && npx prettier --check "src/lib/apis/auths/index.ts" "src/lib/apis/auths/index.test.ts" "src/routes/auth/+page.svelte" "src/routes/auth/guest-flow.test.ts" "src/routes/+layout.svelte" "src/lib/components/layout/Sidebar/UserMenu.svelte" "src/lib/components/layout/Overlay/AccountPending.svelte" && ./.venv/bin/python -m py_compile backend/open_webui/routers/auths.py backend/open_webui/config.py backend/open_webui/main.py

blockingIssues.description: The added portable guest-flow test file still reimplements decision helpers locally instead of exercising the real Svelte auth/bootstrap implementation, so it does not prove the shipped `/auth` and root-layout guest flow cannot regress.

Reviewed evidence still does not demonstrate the enabled guest success path entering the signed-in shell and surviving reload; only blocked fallback behavior is directly evidenced, leaving VAL-GUEST-001/003/005 insufficiently proven.

The reviewed feature does not actually implement the promised scoped diagnostics cleanup: the cited diff only changes `src/routes/auth/guest-flow.test.ts`, while the feature contract targets guest-mode frontend/backend implementation files and the validator contract in `.factory/services.yaml`.

The handoff for the diagnostics-cleanup feature relied on an ad-hoc narrowed pylint invocation that still exited non-zero instead of proving the declared mission-scoped lint contract; this leaves the feature's claimed validator-cleanup outcome unsupported.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify in `auth-fullstack-worker` that helper-only guest-flow tests are not sufficient evidence for auth/bootstrap features; workers must either exercise the real UI/bootstrap implementation or explicitly document why success-path proof is impossible and what substitute evidence closes the gap.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The failing review for `guest-mode-frontend-entry-and-session-bootstrap` found that the worker followed the existing procedure but still shipped local helper tests and blocked-path-only manual evidence, leaving the actual success path unproven.

rejectedObservations.observation: The fixed-port Postgres and ModuleNotFoundError limitations of the legacy auth integration harness should be documented in library guidance.
The services manifest is out of sync because a worker chose to run a different backend lint inspection command than the declared scrutiny validator.
